atomic_propositions.speaker: patient
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: New patient evaluation and management of elevated blood pressure.

atomic_propositions.speaker: patient
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The patient noticed her blood pressure was elevated when she started seeing her new primary care physician last year.

atomic_propositions.speaker: patient
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The patient has been taking medications to manage her blood pressure.

atomic_propositions.speaker: patient
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The patient continues to have difficulty controlling her blood pressure.

atomic_propositions.speaker: patient
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The patient measures her blood pressure at home several times a week.

atomic_propositions.speaker: patient
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The patient has observed her blood pressure increasing, with a recent reading as high as 170 over 90.

atomic_propositions.speaker: patient
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The patient is taking ten milligrams of norvasc daily.

atomic_propositions.speaker: patient
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The patient is taking carvedilol twenty five milligrams twice a day.

atomic_propositions.speaker: patient
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The patient is taking lisinopril before.

atomic_propositions.speaker: patient
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The patient occasionally takes advil for knee pain and back pain.

atomic_propositions.speaker: patient
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The patient enjoys watching football games and frequently consumes pizza, wings, and subs, particularly during games.

atomic_propositions.speaker: patient
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The patient has been trying to cut back on these foods, but it has been difficult.

atomic_propositions.speaker: patient
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The patient reports mild headaches when her blood pressure is high.

atomic_propositions.speaker: patient
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The patient denies chest pain or shortness of breath.

atomic_propositions.speaker: patient
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The patient reports knee pain

atomic_propositions.speaker: patient
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The patient reports mild headaches

atomic_propositions.speaker: clinician
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: Blood pressure is 169/74 mmHg

atomic_propositions.speaker: clinician
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: Heart rate is 88 bpm

atomic_propositions.speaker: clinician
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: Oxygen saturation is 98%

atomic_propositions.speaker: clinician
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: No jugular venous distension

atomic_propositions.speaker: clinician
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: No carotid bruits

atomic_propositions.speaker: clinician
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: Lung sounds are clear bilaterally

atomic_propositions.speaker: clinician
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: No edema noted

atomic_propositions.speaker: clinician
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: Small, one plus pitting edema is present bilaterally

atomic_propositions.speaker: clinician
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: A two over six systolic ejection murmur is heard

atomic_propositions.speaker: clinician
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The patient's primary problem is uncontrolled hypertension

atomic_propositions.speaker: clinician
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: A multifactorial etiology is suggested, potentially including dietary factors, medication adherence, and underlying renal vascular disease

atomic_propositions.speaker: clinician
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: Cardura 4 milligrams once daily is prescribed
